title: Disease/Disability Information - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Disease/Disability Information 12 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions AIDS/HIV OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers education programs to help people understand what to expect with and how to prepare for having and caring for Alzheimer's and other dementias. Courses are offered online and in-person in local communities. 1080 SW Mount Bachelor Drive #100 Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Support group times vary Email (800) 272-3900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers education programs to help people understand what to expect with and how to prepare for having and caring for Alzheimer's and other dementias. Courses are offered online and in-person in local communities. 2615 Portland Road NE Room 2 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Support group times vary Email (800) 272-3900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers education programs to help people understand what to expect with and how to prepare for having and caring for Alzheimer's and other dementias. Courses are offered online and in-person in local communities. 5285 Meadows Road Suite 451 Lake Oswego, OR 97035 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Support group times vary Email (800) 272-3900 247 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMERICAN HEART/AMERICAN STROKE ASSOCIATION AMERICAN HEART ASSOCIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Strives to reduce death and disability from cardiovascular disease through education and public awareness efforts. Website offers information on healthy living, specific cardiovascular conditions, tips for caregivers, workplace health resources, and resources for educators. 7272 Greenville Avenue Dallas, TX 75231 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-5pm, Saturday 7am-3pm (800) 242-8721 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMERICAN HEART/AMERICAN STROKE ASSOCIATION AMERICAN STROKE ASSOCIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Strives to reduce death and disability from stroke through education and public awareness efforts. Website offers information on warning signs, treatment, and recovery from stroke. Also offers listings of local stroke support groups, an online stroke support network for those affected by stroke to share their experiences, and the Stroke Connection E-newsletter. 7272 Greenville Avenue Dallas, TX 75231 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 5am-5pm, Saturday 7am-3pm (888) 478-7653 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAREASSIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance paying for insurance premiums, prescriptions, and medical service co-pays. Assists with 100% payments for a limited number of medical services and medications necessary for HIV treatment for uninsured persons. 800 NE Oregon Street #1105 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: HIV positive Oregonians with income up to 550% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (971) 673-0144 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DIABETES SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about weekly diabetes classes, one-on-one consultations, and support groups. Support groups often include a medical professional speaker and information on exercise, diet, diabetic products and services. Child care is available upon request. Supplies such as meters, strips, and lancets may be available depending on supplies. 1274 Cunningham Lane S Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: People with a pre-diabetes or diabetes diagnosis (Type 1 or Type 2), including parents of children with diabetes Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 11am-3pm Whole Body Sonic sessions: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 11am-3pm or by appointment Email (503) 585-1335 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON SWINDELLS RESOURCE CENTER HELP ME GROW OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supports for families, especially those who experience behavioral, developmental, and complex medical concerns. Connects parents and caregivers to parent education classes, sibling support groups, and inclusive developmental playgroups. 9135 SW Barnes Road Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Families with children 0-5 years and pregnant individuals in any trimester Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (883) 868-4769 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOLVE ME/CFS INITIATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and resources on myalgic encephalomyelitis (ME)/chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS), also known as chronic fatigue and immune dysfunction syndrome (CFIDS), for patients, researchers, and health care providers. Also coordinates patient-centered research aimed at the early detection, objective diagnosis, and effective treatment of ME/CFS. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (704) 364-0016 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON COMMUNITY EDUCATION CLASSES AND EARLY-STAGE PROGRAMS CAREASSIST CAREASSIST CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM DIABETES SUPPORT SERVICES DIABETES SUPPORT SERVICES PROVIDENCE HEALTH AND SERVICES OREGON AND SOUTHWEST WASHINGTON HELP ME GROW OREGON
